entry.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
entry.content:
A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: EU Sanctions Companies in China, Iran for Cyberattacks
entry.content:
These rulings prohibit the entities from entering or doing business in the European Union.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Native Launches With Security Control Plane for Multicloud
entry.content:
The cloud security startup's platform translates and enforces security policies across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle using provider-native controls.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
entry.content:
RSAC Conference Preview: MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Interlock Ransomware Targets Cisco Enterprise Firewalls
entry.content:
The ransomware gang, known for double-extortion attacks, had access to a critical Cisco firewall vulnerability weeks before it was publicly disclosed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
entry.content:
Files on a central cloud server used by the ransomware group highlight a systematic, aggressive attack on network backups as a key TTP.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Hide Infostealer in Copyright Infringement Notices
entry.content:
A phishing campaign targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in various countries uses several evasion techniques to avoid detection.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: CISOs Debate Human Role in AI-Powered Security
entry.content:
The idea of a "human in the loop" in AI deployment was challenged during a security executive panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference this week.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Ransomware's New Era: Moving at AI Speed
entry.content:
Threat actors bypass security tools and use AI to launch faster ransomware attacks that exploit valid credentials and target data
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Targets CI/CD Secrets
entry.content:
A threat actor used the open source security tool to deploy an infostealer into CI/CD workflows and steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, tokens, and other sensitive secrets.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: In Other News: New Android Safeguards, Operation Alice, UK Toughens Cyber Reporting
entry.content:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: vulnerabilities found in KVM devices, Claudy Day Claude vulnerabilities, The Gentlemen ransomware group. The post In Other News: New Android Safeguards, Operation Alice, UK Toughens Cyber Reporting appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Quest KACE Vulnerability Potentially Exploited in Attacks
entry.content:
The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2025-32975 and it may have been exploited in attacks against the education sector. The post Critical Quest KACE Vulnerability Potentially Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Oracle Releases Emergency Patch for Critical Identity Manager Vulnerability
entry.content:
CVE-2026-21992 can be used without authentication for remote code execution and it may have been exploited in the wild. The post Oracle Releases Emergency Patch for Critical Identity Manager Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: QNAP Patches Four Vulnerabilities Exploited at Pwn2Own
entry.content:
The flaws could allow attackers to access sensitive information, execute code, or cause unexpected behavior. The post QNAP Patches Four Vulnerabilities Exploited at Pwn2Own appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Aqua’s Trivy Vulnerability Scanner Hit by Supply Chain Attack
entry.content:
Hackers published a malicious scanner release and replaced tags to point to information-stealer malware. The post Aqua’s Trivy Vulnerability Scanner Hit by Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Chip Services Firm Trio-Tech Says Subsidiary Hit by Ransomware
entry.content:
The semiconductor company says hackers deployed file-encrypting ransomware on the network of a subsidiary in Singapore. The post Chip Services Firm Trio-Tech Says Subsidiary Hit by Ransomware appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: California-based semiconductor testing company reports ransomware attack to SEC
entry.content:
A semiconductor testing company warned regulators that its subsidiary in Singapore suffered a ransomware attack earlier this month.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Education company Kaplan reports data breach impacting more than 230,000
entry.content:
The educational services company Kaplan told state regulators that at least 230,000 people had Social Security and driver’s license numbers leaked following a cybersecurity incident in the fall of 2025.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: FBI warns of Russian, Iranian cyber activity involving messaging platforms
entry.content:
The FBI issued warnings about separate Russian and Iranian cyber campaigns involving social media messaging platforms like Signal and Telegram.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hacker walks away with $24.5 million after breaching Resolv DeFi platform
entry.content:
In a message to the attacker on the blockchain, Resolv offered the person 10% of the $24.5 million in ETH if they returned the rest and ceased all further activity with the exploited funds.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
entry.content:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
entry.content:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
entry.content:
A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE
entry.content:
Trinity Health and the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center are notifying patients about potential unauthorized access to patient data by […] The post Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required
entry.content:
According to the Paubox 2026 Healthcare Email Security Report, in 2025, 170 email-related data breaches were reported to the HHS’ […] The post Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: CISA Advises U.S. Organizations to Harden Microsoft Intune Following Stryker Data Wiping Attack
entry.content:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is urging U.S. organizations to strengthen administrative controls for the Intune endpoint […] The post CISA Advises U.S. Organizations to Harden Microsoft Intune Following Stryker Data Wiping Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Final Rule Implementing HIPAA Security Rule Updates Edges Closer
entry.content:
The HIPAA Security Rule update proposed by OCR in the final days of the Biden administration is only two months […] The post Final Rule Implementing HIPAA Security Rule Updates Edges Closer appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Essen Medical Associates Agree to $4 Million Settlement to Resolve Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Essen Medical Associates has agreed to pay $4,000,000 to resolve class action litigation over a March 2023 cyberattack and data […] The post Essen Medical Associates Agree to $4 Million Settlement to Resolve Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Navia Benefit Solutions Discloses Data Breach Affecting 2.7 Million Individuals
entry.content:
Over a three-week period between December 2025 and January 2026, hackers had access to the network of a Washington-based employee […] The post Navia Benefit Solutions Discloses Data Breach Affecting 2.7 Million Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Akeela Data Breach Settlement Gets First Nod from the Court
entry.content:
In June of last year, we reported that a settlement had been agreed to resolve a class action lawsuit against […] The post Akeela Data Breach Settlement Gets First Nod from the Court appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Balance Autism Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Balance Autism has agreed to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from a security incident that exposed patient information. Altoona, […] The post Balance Autism Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Strengthening Cybersecurity in Canada’s Municipal Sector: A Verified Analysis
entry.content:
From the Nonprofit Tech Support: When the City of Hamilton suffered a ransomware attack on February 25, 2024, it marked a sobering milestone in Canadian municipal cybersecurity. The attack crippled roughly 80% of Hamilton’s network, impacting services from business licensing to the fire department’s records. Attackers demanded an $18.5 million ransom, which the city refused... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
entry.content:
Robert Lemos writes: An open server hosted on a German cloud provider’s systems has been discovered, containing the entire toolset of a member of the Beast ransomware group. The find exposes the tactics, techniques, and procedures of the threat actor, but also reveals that Beast shares many of those TTPs with other ransomware gangs. According... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Weill Cornell Medicine discloses an insider data breach
entry.content:
On February 23, HHS received a breach submission from Weill Cornell Medicine in New York. The submission reported that 516 patients were affected by an incident involving Unauthorized Access/Disclosure of data in Electronic Medical Records (EMR). DataBreaches emailed the hospital to request an explanation and received the following statement from a spokesperson: After thorough investigation,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: AU: Fairfield Council obtains injunction against unknown threat actors in ransomware incident
entry.content:
Anthony Segaert reports: A western Sydney council is communicating with anonymous hackers by sending Dropbox links into a chatroom, after it suffered a major data breach. In October last year, Fairfield Council’s servers – which contained personal, financial and property information about councillors, ratepayers, residents and staff – were illegally accessed by hackers, who are... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hackers target schools, towns in alarming attacks. Why aren’t more using New Jersey’s MS-ISAC ybersecurity service?
entry.content:
If you’re asking, “What MS-ISAC service?”, you’re not alone. Brianna Kudisch reports: A nationwide data breach exposing millions of K-12 students’ information, including kids in Cranford and Millburn. […] In November 2025, New Jersey signed on as a statewide member of the Multi-State Information Sharing and Analysis Center. It pays $795,000 for its annual membership, according to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: GitHub is starting to have a real malware problem
entry.content:
Catalin Cimpanu reports: GitHub is slowly becoming a very dangerous website as more and more threat actors are starting to use it to host and distribute malware disguised as legitimate software repositories. What started as an infrequent sighting in early 2024 is now at the center of an increasing number of infosec and malware reports. The tactic... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: 3.7 Million Telehealth Patients Allegedly Affected By Two Recent Breaches
entry.content:
He hasn’t attracted much attention or media coverage yet, and he doesn’t have any leak site or Telegram account. However, those reporting breaches involving patient data should note a threat actor known as “Stuckin2019” (or simply “Stuck”). Two of his recent attacks allegedly affected telehealth entities and 3.7 million patients. OpenLoop Health On January 7,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: If threat actors gave you a chance to redact the patient data they hacked before they leak it, would you take them up on the offer? Read about the Woundtech incident.
entry.content:
On January 2, while many were still greeting each other with “Happy New Year,” Wound Technology Network (“Woundtech”), a Florida-headquartered mobile wound treatment provider, was not off to a great start for the year. On or about December 6, 2025, they had become aware of unusual activity in their network. An investigation confirmed that unauthorized... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hackers increasingly target school districts
entry.content:
Nolan Keegan reports: Hackers are increasingly treating school districts like easy ATMs for personal data, and Bronx families are squarely in the crosshairs. A recent Turn To Tara segment on News 12 New York spotlighted a sharp jump in cyberattacks on schools and highlighted warnings from Check Point security analysts about exposed Social Security numbers,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Initial Access Broker sentenced to 81 months in prison for enabling Yanluowang ransomware gang
entry.content:
A press release from the U.S. Department of Justice about a Russian initial access broker who pleaded guilty in November, 2025: A court in the Southern District of Indiana today sentenced a Russian citizen, Aleksei Volkov, to 81 months in prison for assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware group, in carrying out numerous... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Microsoft Azure Monitor alerts abused for callback phishing attacks
entry.content:
Microsoft Azure Monitor alerts are being abused to send callback phishing emails that impersonate warnings from the Microsoft Security Team about unauthorized charges on your account. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Trivy vulnerability scanner breach pushed infostealer via GitHub Actions
entry.content:
The Trivy vulnerability scanner was compromised in a supply-chain attack by threat actors known as TeamPCP, which distributed credential-stealing malware through official releases and GitHub Actions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CISA orders feds to patch DarkSword iOS flaws exploited attacks
entry.content:
CISA ordered U.S. government agencies to patch three iOS vulnerabilities targeted in cryptocurrency theft and cyberespionage attacks using the DarkSword exploit kit. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: FBI warns of Handala hackers using Telegram in malware attacks
entry.content:
The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned network defenders that Iranian hackers linked to the country's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) are using Telegram in malware attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Trivy supply-chain attack spreads to Docker, GitHub repos
entry.content:
The TeamPCP hackers behind the Trivy supply-chain attack continued to target Aqua Security, pushing malicious Docker images and hijacking the company's GitHub organization to tamper with dozens of repositories. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Crunchyroll probes breach after hacker claims to steal 6.8M users' data
entry.content:
Popular anime streaming platform Crunchyroll is investigating a breach after hackers claimed to have stolen personal information for approximately 6.8 million people. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: TeamPCP deploys Iran-targeted wiper in Kubernetes attacks
entry.content:
The TeamPCP hacking group is targeting Kubernetes clusters with a malicious script that wipes all machines when it detects systems configured for Iran. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Mazda discloses security breach exposing employee and partner data
entry.content:
Mazda Motor Corporation (Mazda) announced that information belonging to its employees and business partners had been exposed in a security incident detected last December. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.content:
Colina Salud reported a ransomware. Attributed to TheGentlemen.
